event_name: Leo Platform npm Miasma / Phantom Gyp Compromise
confidence_reason: StepSecurity documented the affected package list and publish burst, and npm registry metadata confirms representative publish timestamps for multiple packages in that burst.

artifact_analysis.execution_trigger: npm install via binding.gyp / node-gyp shell expansion
payload_behavior: downloads Bun v1.3.13 from GitHub Releases
encrypts and commits stolen data to GitHub via GraphQL
publishes additional npm packages through bypass_2fa

provenance.campaign_relation: likely child event of the Miasma / Mini Shai-Hulud activity based on identical Phantom Gyp and payload fingerprints

network_patterns: GitHub GraphQL API exfiltration with victim token

lockfile_hunts: Search lockfiles and package cache records for the listed Leo Platform package versions.
filesystem_hunts: Look for binding.gyp plus payload selectors such as Runner.Worker or bypass_2fa in package caches and extracted tarballs.
process_hunts: Check endpoint or CI telemetry for Bun execution, Runner.Worker memory access, or node-gyp shell expansion during dependency install.
network_hunts: Review outbound connections to GitHub GraphQL and Bun release URLs during npm install windows.
ci_cd_hunts: Audit GitHub Actions logs and workflow diffs for post-install secret theft and workflow write activity.
registry_hunts: Confirm whether internal mirrors cached the malicious package versions before cleanup.

open_questions: A public vendor statement or cleanup timeline from Leo Platform was not located during this refresh.

defender_takeaways.detection: Treat any install of the listed versions as a potential credential-theft event and search both package evidence and GitHub/cloud follow-on activity.
defender_takeaways.hunting: Use package-version selectors together with GitHub GraphQL, Bun download, and Runner.Worker memory-access patterns to separate documentation references from true execution.
defender_takeaways.remediation: Rebuild from clean dependencies and rotate every credential reachable from affected runners or workstations.
defender_takeaways.prevention: Disable unnecessary install-time script execution, restrict publish rights, and isolate CI runner trust boundaries.

containment_complete: Affected package versions removed from lockfiles, caches, and internal mirrors
eradication_complete: Hosts and runners rebuilt from clean dependencies
recovery_complete: GitHub, npm, cloud, Vault, and password-manager credentials rotated
closure_required: No unexplained GitHub or cloud write activity remains in the exposure window
